key_insight: Dendrite stripping in sodium all-solid-state batteries is asymmetric and leaves isolated Na at grain-boundary junctions that reactivates in later cycles, accelerating penetration.
engineering_relevance: Controlling grain-boundary geometry, electrolyte microstructure, and anode chemistry could suppress residual Na and improve cycle stability in solid-state Na batteries.
method: Phase-field simulation of dendrite evolution during cycling
Density functional theory used to inform model energetics
Compared pure Na and Na-Sb alloy anodes in polycrystalline Na3SbS4 electrolyte
evidence_signal: The abstract reports model-based visualization and mechanistic analysis of dendrite behavior across charging and discharging, plus sensitivity to voltage, microstructure, and anode composition.
watch_item: Because the conclusions come from simulation-informed interpretation, real-cell validation is still needed to confirm how strongly the predicted residual Na behavior governs failure.
paper_title: Phase-Field Simulation of Dendrite Evolution in All-Solid-State Sodium Batteries during Cycling
